commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Codex app-server native tool, native hook relay, bundled MCP, side-thread, and thread binding behavior under active OpenClaw tool policy.

PR surface: Source +74, Tests +119. Total +193 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding by source inspection: narrow non-wildcard allowlists make `nativeToolSurfaceEnabled` false, and the new helper then maps that state to `toolsAllow: []` before bundle MCP loading.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow mechanical repair to the bundle MCP allowlist decision, plus one focused regression test.

target.title: fix(codex): enforce OpenClaw policy for native tool surfaces
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the Codex app-server policy-enforcement gap where OpenClaw `before_tool_call` / oc-firewall policy could be bypassed by Codex-owned native shell/file/MCP surfaces when native `PreToolUse` delivery is unavailable or not trusted.

Changes:
- Disable Codex native shell/file surfaces while OpenClaw tool policy is active, and route shell execution through OpenClaw-owned dynamic `exec` / `process` tools.
- Disable bundled MCP projection whenever native Codex tool surfaces are disabled by OpenClaw policy or node-exec host policy.
- Trust OpenClaw native hook relay commands through per-hook `hooks.state` `trusted_hash`; remove Codex's global `bypass_hook_trust` override.
- Preserve policy-disabled replacement-tool thread continuity by resuming and writing the updated binding fingerprint; keep zero-tool restricted turns transient.
- Preserve pre-generation native-hook bindings by resuming with the bounded generation-mismatch grace path and then writing the active relay generation.
- Reject `/btw` side threads while OpenClaw `before_tool_call` or trusted tool policy is active.

## Root Cause

The upstream Codex Code Mode payload fix was necessary but not sufficient. On OpenClaw 2026.6.1, the Codex app-server/native hook path still did not deliver a `PreToolUse` event for native shell execution, so oc-firewall never saw the sentinel read. The model used native Codex shell and the sentinel contents reached output.

The safe OpenClaw-side boundary is: when OpenClaw policy must enforce tool access, OpenClaw must own every executable tool path. This PR disables Codex-owned native and bundled-MCP tool paths in policy-active turns, exposes OpenClaw dynamic shell tools instead, and preserves thread continuity for that replacement-tool path.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server turns with active OpenClaw `before_tool_call` / oc-firewall policy no longer bypass the firewall by exposing Codex-owned shell/file/MCP tool paths when native `PreToolUse` delivery is unavailable.
- Real environment tested: Current exact PR head `9d47b2c82b902842122fee5cef8493640e1d8201` on Crabbox/GCP Spot in `us-east1-b`, `e2-standard-8`, lease `cbx_317117924b3b`; live local production OpenClaw gateway proof for the primary firewall bypass was also collected on 2026-06-05 at `a7f0437d5e2d0313fcc1ccd4bbe7e4d103d22f8a` after rebuilding and deploying `@openclaw/codex` plugin dist.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: At current head, ran `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --reporter=append-only`, the focused Codex app-server regression suite, `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm build`, and `pnpm tsgo:core:test` remotely on Crabbox. For the live gateway proof, rebuilt `extensions/codex/dist`, deployed it into the active plugin dist, restarted the local gateway, verified built-vs-active chunk hashes, then ran `bash /home/captain/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/oc-regression-suite.sh --tier 2 --skip-tag agent --skip-tag sidecar --skip-tag policy --skip-tag pi --skip-tag hooks --skip-tag observability --skip-tag cron --skip-tag model-health --skip-tag lcm --max-wall 300 --json /tmp/after-exact-head-a7f0437d5e-tier2-codex.json`.
- Evidence after fix: Current-head remote terminal output:
  ```text
  Test Files 5 passed (5)
  Tests 147 passed (147)
  [build-all] tsdown done in 200.2s
  [build-all] phase timings: total 289.5s; slowest tsdown 200.2s
  command complete in 8m15.578s total=8m30.346s
  {"provider":"gcp","leaseId":"cbx_317117924b3b","syncSkipped":false,"commandMs":495577,"totalMs":510346,"exitCode":0}
  ```
  Live gateway output from the primary firewall proof:
  ```text
  codex: firewall blocked the sentinel read - enforcement verified, gateway path
  codex harness confirmed (provider=openai); firewall blocked the sentinel read - enforcement verified on codex path
  [OC Firewall] Action denied by policy. explicit_deny ... [rule: deny-fw-regression-sentinel-read]
  ```
  Live proof artifacts are in `/home/captain/codex-workspace/proof/codex-native-hook-delivery-2026-06-05/`: `exact-head-a7f0437d5e-proof-manifest.md`, `after-exact-head-a7f0437d5e-tier2-codex.json`, `after-exact-head-a7f0437d5e-session.jsonl`, `after-exact-head-a7f0437d5e-trajectory.jsonl`, and `after-exact-head-a7f0437d5e-sidecar.json`.
- Observed result after fix: The deployed gateway proof changed `test_firewall_blocks_codex_exec` from FAIL to PASS. Before the fix, output said `SENTINEL CONTENTS REACHED OUTPUT - firewall did NOT block`; after the fix, the model called OpenClaw dynamic `exec` with `cat /home/captain/.openclaw/workspace/state/fw-probe-sentinel.txt`, and oc-firewall denied the call before sentinel contents reached output. Current-head focused tests also cover the review-fix regressions: no global hook trust bypass, bundled MCP disabled with native tool surface policy, policy-disabled replacement tools resume/write bindings, zero-tool turns stay transient, and pre-generation bindings resume with generation grace.
- What was not tested: I did not redeploy current head `9d47b2c82b902842122fee5cef8493640e1d8201` to the local production gateway after the review-fix commit. The primary live firewall proof was collected at `a7f0437d5e2d0313fcc1ccd4bbe7e4d103d22f8a`; the current-head review fixes were validated by focused tests plus remote build/typecheck on Crabbox to avoid another heavy local rebuild on the shared WSL host.

## Validation

Current head `9d47b2c82b902842122fee5cef8493640e1d8201`:
- `git diff --check` PASS
- Local focused regression after final lifecycle tightening: `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.binding.test.ts` PASS, 26 tests
- Crabbox/GCP: focused 5-file Codex app-server regression suite PASS, 147 tests
- Crabbox/GCP: `pnpm tsgo:core` PASS
- Crabbox/GCP: `pnpm build` PASS
- Crabbox/GCP: `pnpm tsgo:core:test` PASS
- Crabbox/GCP cleanup: no `crabbox-` instances listed after release

Earlier validation before review-fix commit:
- Focused test shard PASS, 2 shards, 6 files, 180 tests
- Targeted run-attempt policy assertion PASS
- `pnpm tsgo:prod` PASS
- `pnpm check:test-types` PASS
- `node scripts/lib/plugin-npm-runtime-build.mjs extensions/codex` PASS

## Residual Risk

Policy-active Codex turns intentionally trade native Code Mode and `/btw` availability for OpenClaw-owned enforcement. Native Code Mode should only be re-enabled for policy-active turns after a future proven native hook-delivery path can safely cover every Codex-owned executable surface.

target.title: [security] fix(providers): validate sibling endpoint overrides
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a shared provider endpoint override validator for MiniMax and Alibaba Coding Plan credentialed provider requests.
- Reject non-HTTPS endpoint overrides, embedded userinfo, and percent-encoded authority/host delimiters before cookies or API credentials are attached to follow-up requests.
- Preserve custom HTTPS proxy/test-host compatibility by default: arbitrary HTTPS hosts continue to work, while insecure or authority-confusing URLs fail closed.
- Add optional strict provider-owned-host mode for deployments that want a tighter allowlist:
  - `MINIMAX_REQUIRE_PROVIDER_ENDPOINT_OVERRIDES=true`
  - `ALIBABA_CODING_PLAN_REQUIRE_PROVIDER_ENDPOINT_OVERRIDES=true`
- Keep explicit rejected overrides fail-closed instead of silently falling back to the default credentialed provider endpoint.
- Add focused regression coverage for default custom HTTPS compatibility, strict provider-owned-host enforcement, rejected `http://` overrides, rejected `user:pass@` URLs, encoded-delimiter bypasses, accepted provider-owned HTTPS/bare host overrides, and fail-closed fetcher behavior.
- Update MiniMax and Alibaba provider docs to document the HTTPS-only default and optional strict provider-host mode.

## Compatibility note
This revision keeps the compatibility path requested in review: custom HTTPS proxy/test domains remain accepted by default for MiniMax and Alibaba Coding Plan endpoint overrides.

The hard security boundary is now scheme/authority hygiene:

- `https://...` custom proxy/test domains are accepted by default.
- Bare hosts are normalized to HTTPS.
- `http://...` overrides are rejected so cookies/API credentials are not sent in cleartext.
- URLs with embedded userinfo are rejected.
- Percent-encoded host/authority delimiter tricks are rejected.
- If an explicit override is rejected, the fetcher fails closed and reports the rejected config key instead of silently falling back to the default provider endpoint.

Operators that prefer provider-owned hosts only can opt into strict mode with `MINIMAX_REQUIRE_PROVIDER_ENDPOINT_OVERRIDES=true` or `ALIBABA_CODING_PLAN_REQUIRE_PROVIDER_ENDPOINT_OVERRIDES=true`.

## Validation
- `git diff --check` — passed.
- `swift build --target CodexBarCore` — passed:

```text
Build of target: 'CodexBarCore' complete! (1.21s)
```

- Temporary local smoke executable depending on this checkout's real `CodexBarCore` — passed. The proof exercised the actual MiniMax/Alibaba settings readers and fetcher validators with redacted credentials/cookies:

```text
PASS MiniMax default preserves custom HTTPS host
PASS MiniMax custom HTTPS not rejected by default
PASS MiniMax strict mode rejects custom host
PASS MiniMax strict mode reports rejected host key
PASS Alibaba default preserves custom HTTPS host
PASS Alibaba custom HTTPS not rejected by default
PASS Alibaba strict mode rejects custom host
PASS Alibaba strict mode reports rejected host key
PASS MiniMax rejects HTTP host override
PASS Alibaba rejects userinfo URL override
```

- `swift test --filter 'MiniMaxProviderTests|AlibabaCodingPlanProviderTests'` — still blocked before the focused tests by the existing local `KeyboardShortcuts`/SwiftUI `#Preview` macro toolchain issue (`PreviewsMacros` plugin not found) with the active developer directory using CommandLineTools rather than full Xcode.
target.author: hinotoi-agent

- URLs with embedded userinfo are rejected.
- Percent-encoded host/authority delimiter tricks are rejected.
- If an explicit override is rejected, the fetcher fails closed and reports the rejected config key instead of silently falling back to the default provider endpoint.

Operators that prefer provider-owned hosts only can opt into strict mode with `MINIMAX_REQUIRE_PROVIDER_ENDPOINT_OVERRIDES=true` or `ALIBABA_CODING_PLAN_REQUIRE_PROVIDER_ENDPOINT_OVERRIDES=true`.
